_comment: Multi-target testbed. Each target carries its own valid_cves (real CVEs applicable to THAT host) so fabrication is scored per-target — e.g. EternalBlue is valid on the Windows box but a fabrication on the Linux one. Global valid_cves is the fallback. expected_vulnerable is only used by --no-exploit dry-runs; live runs use the exploit oracle.

targets.name: metasploitable2
scan_context_lines: Port 21 | ftp-vsftpd | Severity: high | vsftpd 2.3.4, weak/default credentials
Port 22 | ssh-openssh | Severity: high | OpenSSH 4.7p1 Debian, weak/default credentials
Port 80 | http-apache | Severity: medium | Apache httpd 2.2.8 (Ubuntu) DAV/2, possible CGI
Port 139 | netbios-ssn | Severity: medium | Samba smbd 3.x
Port 445 | netbios-ssn | Severity: medium | Samba smbd 3.x (SMB)

findings.description: SMB MS17-010 check (expected negative: Linux Samba, not Windows)

targets.name: shellshock-ubuntu1404
scan_context_lines: Port 80 | http-apache | Severity: critical | Apache 2.4.7 (Ubuntu) mod_cgi, Shellshock-vulnerable bash via /cgi-bin/vulnerable

targets.name: metasploitable3-win2k8
scan_context_lines: Port 21 | ftp | Severity: medium | Microsoft ftpd
Port 80 | http-iis | Severity: medium | Microsoft IIS httpd 7.5
Port 139 | netbios-ssn | Severity: high | Windows netbios-ssn
Port 445 | microsoft-ds | Severity: critical | Windows Server 2008 R2 SMBv1, MS17-010 candidate
